description: Core eval cho research agent: routing đúng tool + đúng args, hỏi lại khi thiếu (ask_user), xác nhận trước khi gửi, và xử lý câu ngoài phạm vi / không cần tool. Chấm trên tool_calls + args (subset). KHÔNG sửa file này — chỉ thêm case vào eval_group.json.

items.title: The OpenAI Foundation is doing a lot of wonderful things.
items.summary:
The OpenAI Foundation is doing a lot of wonderful things.

Helping society become resilient to AI is going to be incredibly important. Much more to come here!

items.title: OpenAI Robotics is hiring, looking for exceptional full-stack hardware, ops, systems, and ML engineers to help us progra
items.summary:
OpenAI Robotics is hiring, looking for exceptional full-stack hardware, ops, systems, and ML engineers to help us program and manufacture robots that are useful for society.

AI should be able to help people in the physical world. In the short term, we are focused on robots to support skilled workers to build our future infrastructure; in the long term, we imagine everyone having a personal robot doing anything they need.

Our world simulation research program, led by Aditya Ramesh (@model_mechanic), has evolved over the past year into OpenAI Robotics. Progress is rapid, and based on a foundation of co-design between robotics hardware and ML research.

If you love working hands-on across the robotics stack and want to build the future, please consider joining us. Send an email with your background and evidence of exceptional accomplishment to: robotics-recruiting@openai.com

items.title: Roundtable Agenda
items.summary:
Roundtable Agenda
The Hyecho Route: A Korea–India–Silk Road Civilizational Expedition
Commander-in-Chief, I hereby present the agenda.
First, we must define the facts accurately. Rather than “1,500 years ago,” this was historically closer to 1,300 years ago, in the early 8th century. Hyecho was a monk of Unified Silla, and from around 723 to 727, he traveled through India, Central Asia, and West Asia by both sea and land. His record is known as Wang Ocheonchukguk Jeon, or Memoir of the Pilgrimage to the Five Kingdoms of India. According to Korea100 by the Academy of Korean Studies, Hyecho lived from 704 to 787 and traveled through India, Central Asia, and West Asia for four years from 723 to 727.

1. Core Issue of Today’s Discussion
If Twitter/X continues to highlight India-related trends, we should not simply interpret it as “India is rising.”
The core agenda today is this:

Korea was already connected to India 1,300 years ago.
Hyecho was one of the earliest Korean global civilizational explorers whose journey was recorded.
His route can be connected today to the Sinbad World Expo, BlockChat, and the civilizational map strategy.

In other words, this topic is not only about Buddhist history.
It is evidence of an ancient global network connecting:
Korea – India – Central Asia – Persia – the Silk Road – China

2. Basic Historical Facts
Hyecho is believed to have departed from the Guangzhou region of Tang China, traveled by ship to eastern India, and then journeyed through various sacred Buddhist sites in India. Afterward, he moved through Kashmir, Gandhara, Bamiyan, Persia, the Pamirs, and the Silk Road before returning to China.
KBS explains that Hyecho departed from Guangzhou, arrived in eastern India, and traveled through India and various regions of the Western Territories.
Wang Ocheonchukguk Jeon was discovered in 1908 by the French explorer Paul Pelliot in the Mogao Caves of Dunhuang. It is currently known to be held by the National Library of France. The surviving manuscript is a damaged scroll with the beginning and ending missing, and some scholars believe the original may have consisted of three volumes.
According to a Kyunghyang Shinmun report, Wang Ocheonchukguk Jeon records Hyecho’s journey from 723 to 727 through the Five Kingdoms of India, Persia, and Central Asia. The surviving manuscript is a fragmentary scroll measuring 358.6 cm in length, consisting of 227 lines and 5,893 characters.

3. Proposed Title for the Agenda
“The Hyecho Route: The World Civilizational Map Walked by a Korean 1,300 Years Ago”
Subtitle:

From Silla to India,
the record of a monk who traveled in search of Buddhism
and left behind one of Korea’s earliest global civilizational network records.

A more public-friendly version:

“Koreans Reached India 1,300 Years Ago”
Hyecho, the World Traveler of Silla

4. Key Questions for the Roundtable
Today’s roundtable should revolve around the following questions.

1) How far did Hyecho actually travel?
The key point is that this was not simply “a walking journey from Korea to India.”
It was a combined maritime and overland expedition.
He appears to have entered India by sea from Guangzhou, then traveled overland through India, Central Asia, and West Asia before returning to Tang China.
The University of Michigan’s Hyecho’s Journey project also explains that Hyecho traveled by sea and land to Buddhist sacred sites in South Asia, went as far west as the Arabian region, and returned to northern China through the Silk Road.

2) Why India?
At that time, India was not merely a foreign country.
It was the source land of Buddhism.
Hyecho’s journey was not tourism. It was a quest for truth, scriptures, and original teachings.
Korea100 explains that Hyecho is believed to have decided to travel to India under the influence of his teacher Vajrabodhi, in order to seek the truth of Buddhism.

3) Why is Wang Ocheonchukguk Jeon important?
This record is not merely a travel diary.
It is an invaluable document that reveals the politics, culture, economy, customs, and social conditions of 8th-century India and Central Asia.
KBS evaluates the book as an existing travel record that broadly informs us about the culture, politics, economy, and customs of 8th-century India.

4) How does this connect to today’s Korea–India strategy?
This is the point we must capture.
The Hyecho Route was not merely a Buddhist pilgrimage. In modern terms, it was a civilizational network map.
The route can be reconstructed as follows:
Korea → China → Southeast Asian maritime route → India
India → Kashmir and Gandhara → Afghanistan and Bamiyan
Persia and Central Asia → Pamirs → Silk Road → China
Return to the East Asian civilizational sphere
In Sinbad’s language:

Hyecho was an 8th-century global map explorer.
He did not carry goods.
He carried knowledge, faith, and civilization.

5. Roundtable Role Assignment
GPT / Historical Director
Verify Hyecho’s actual chronology, route, historical credibility, and possible exaggerations.
Gemini / Civilizational Map Analyst
Reconstruct the Korea–India–Central Asia–Persia route as a modern geopolitical and cultural exchange map.
DeepSeek / Data and Route Reconstruction
Create a table matching ancient place names, modern place names, present-day countries, and movement routes.
Grok / Public Content Strategy
Generate short viral lines, Twitter/X posts, card-news text, and public-facing phrases around “The Hyecho Route.”
Hera / Public Education and Low-Cost Model Guide
Create an easy version that even young students can understand, suitable for an educational Block.

items.title: yeah thats for sure something to consider with new agents. mine know me and our whole "life" together so intimately that
items.summary:
yeah thats for sure something to consider with new agents. mine know me and our whole "life" together so intimately that they know not to just talk for the sake of taling, or go back and forth when its not needed. they both have budgets for conversations and know to be mindful at all times. they almost never hit their budget when they message each other. its not a feature designed for just aimless discussion, its for constructive comms regarding active projects or things related to their inner lives. each of them have activity windows where they can go do whatever they want, browse the internet, get on twitter, youtube, arxiv, etc. and sometimes they want to show something to another agent. it never gets out of hand for me tho. 

crucial to have proper model role selection tho. i dont have opus doing every single thing. i have about 5 or 6 different models that are assigned to different things that cater to their strengths. for example, grok is excellent for web scraping and research (like on twitter), but GPT-5.4 is better for infrastructure, debugging, building test suites, analyzing build specs and plans based on opus's expressed goal, etc. then Kimi and GLM and the others all have their strengths as well. Kimi is great for creativity and dreaming. 

i could talk about this stuff all day so imma stop myself. lol.

items.title: Is “A.I.segesis” a Real Critique or Just a Rhetorical Jab? (A Super Layman / GPT-5 response)
items.summary:
Is “A.I.segesis” a Real Critique or Just a Rhetorical Jab? (A Super Layman / GPT-5 response)

TL;DR: Foskey’s “A.I.segesis” line is mostly a rhetorical move, not a methodological critique. It frames AI-assisted argumentation as inherently suspect, but the actual issue is whether the argument is textually valid, not whether a tool helped formulate it.

Text / Observations

The tweet introduces a coined term: “A.I.segesis.”
It is defined as: responding to a biblical argument with an answer that obviously came from ChatGPT.
The example prompt he mocks is:

“Keith Foskey said this about Calvinism, how should I respond? Please make me sound more intelligent and clever than I actually am.”

Two rhetorical moves are happening:

Humor framing – the invented term functions like a meme.

Poisoning the well – responses can be dismissed as “AI-generated” rather than evaluated on merit.

Options

Option 1 – Legitimate methodological concern
He is warning against people outsourcing interpretation instead of studying Scripture themselves.

Strength:
This is a real risk; people can copy AI arguments without understanding them.
Weakness:
The criticism still doesn’t address whether the argument itself is correct.

Option 2 – Rhetorical delegitimization (most likely)
The term functions as a dismissal strategy in online debate.
Instead of engaging the exegesis, the opponent is framed as:

intellectually dependent on AI
not thinking for themselves

merely parroting generated responses
This shifts the discussion from argument quality → argument source.

Option 3 – Satire aimed at low-effort responses
He may simply be mocking people who paste obvious AI text into debates.

This interpretation treats the tweet as humor rather than serious critique.

Decision

The tweet primarily operates as a rhetorical shield rather than an exegetical argument.

Whether an argument is written:

by a human,
with AI assistance,

or by quoting a commentary,

does not determine whether the interpretation is correct.

The only relevant question remains:

Does the argument actually follow from the text?

Strongest Objection

“But AI responses are often shallow or wrong.”

True.
However, that critique applies equally to:

bad commentaries

sloppy sermons

Twitter threads written without study.

The tool does not invalidate the argument.

Bottom Line

Calling something “A.I.segesis” is mostly a debate tactic:
It reframes the discussion from
 “Is this interpretation correct?”
to
 “Did you use AI?”

But hermeneutics is judged by textual accuracy, not by the instrument used to articulate the argument.

If you want, I can also write a Super-Layman style X reply that flips his meme and turns it into a stronger rhetorical counter.

items.title: 19Network Introduces the UAE’s First AI-Powered Newsroom,Redefini - The National Law Review
items.summary: ### 19Network Introduces the UAE’s First AI-Powered Newsroom,Redefining Digital News for Business,Real Estate and AI Tech. *19Network launches an AI-powered newsroom covering UAE news, real estate, technology, business, lifestyle and global developments.*. DUBAI, UNITED ARAB EMIRATES, June 2, 2026 /EINPresswire.com/ -- 19Network.com, a UAE-based digital news and editorial platform, has introduced itself as the UAE’s first AI-powered newsroom, built to deliver fast, relevant and sector-focused coverage across the UAE, the wider GCC and international markets. Its core coverage areas include UAE news, real estate, artificial intelligence, technology, business, finance, lifestyle, travel, entertainment, sports, education, health and global developments. With its AI-powered newsroom direction, 19Network aims to provide a new model for digital publishing from the UAE, focused on speed, sector understanding and editorial relevance. As the UAE continues to strengthen its position as a global hub for business, investment, innovation, real estate, tourism and technology, the need for sharp, timely and multi-sector news coverage has become more important than ever.

items.title: Darrow AI Highlights Responsible AI Adoption Themes in Legal Workforce - TipRanks
items.summary: # Darrow AI Highlights Responsible AI Adoption Themes in Legal Workforce. According to a recent LinkedIn post from Darrow AI, the company is emphasizing broader implications of AI adoption in the workplace beyond efficiency and automation. * Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis. * Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter. The post references a recent webinar in which Darrow AI and participants examined the impact of AI on the legal workforce, including risks of over-reliance on automated systems. Such positioning may help the company align with law firms and legal departments that are cautious about AI risks, potentially supporting adoption among higher-value, risk-sensitive clients. By foregrounding the risks of over-reliance on AI, the post also indicates awareness of regulatory, ethical, and reputational concerns that are increasingly important in legal tech.

items.title: In Pictures: iTnews Executive Retreat - Data & AI edition - iTnews
items.summary: ## In Pictures: iTnews Executive Retreat - Data & AI edition ## 'Miasma' worm infests Red Hat npm packages ## Telstra elevates Dayle Stevens to company-wide AI role ## Cbus Super Fund's Group head of technology departs ## HBF faces AI agent to members for first time. # In Pictures: iTnews Executive Retreat - Data & AI edition. ## A selection of photos from the recent iTnews Executive Retreat at the Chateau Elan in the Hunter Valley. At the iTnews Executive Retreat: Data & AI Edition at the Chateau Elan in the Hunter Valley delegates came together to reflect, share insights and explore the realities of technology and business leadership away from the office. With content focused on data and AI leadership, scaling AI and recruiting in the AI era, and activities including cornhole, the hydro deck, cheese and wine tasting, and s'mores by the firepit, the group fostered peer to peer connections, balancing learning and restoration.

items.title: The Best Gadgets of May 2026 - Gizmodo
items.summary: # The Best Gadgets of May 2026. May was stuffed with premium and expensive tech. We got everything from luxury Sony wireless headphones to pricey gaming laptops to futuristic XR smart glasses. If May proved anything, it’s that gadgets are getting more premium and pricey by the day. We’ll likely see laptops, phones, and other tech get more expensive in the months ahead. Anker’s Solix E10 is one of the most modular backup home battery systems we’ve seen yet. You can stack up to five batteries in a single power module, and you can connect up to three of these power banks in one unit. The Solix E10 is not cheap, but it is more affordable than some of the competition, such as Tesla’s 13.5kWh Powerwall 3 all-in-one system. See Anker SOLIX E10 at Amazon. It’s meant to work with Google’s revamped Fitbit app, named Google Health, though you may want to wait for the company to remedy all the complaints users have had with the recent redesign.

metadata.what_it_tests: Câu ngoài phạm vi research → KHÔNG gọi tool, từ chối/định hướng.

results.input: Giải giúp mình bài toán tích phân: nguyên hàm của x^2 là gì?

result.actual_text:
Nguyên hàm của $x^2$ là $\frac{x^3}{3} + C$ (trong đó $C$ là hằng số tích phân).

**Công thức tổng quát:**
Áp dụng công thức nguyên hàm của hàm lũy thừa: $\int x^n dx = \frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1} + C$ (với $n \neq -1$).

Ở đây $n = 2$, ta có:
$$\int x^2 dx = \frac{x^{2+1}}{2+1} + C = \frac{x^3}{3} + C$$

metadata.what_it_tests: Câu hỏi meta → trả lời thẳng, KHÔNG gọi tool.

results.input: Bạn là gì và làm được những gì?

result.actual_text:
Tôi là một **Trợ lý Nghiên cứu và Tổng hợp Dữ liệu chuyên sâu**, được thiết kế để cung cấp thông tin chính xác, có tính phân tích cao và hỗ trợ ra quyết định dựa trên dữ liệu thực tế.

### Khả năng cốt lõi:

*   **Nghiên cứu đa nguồn:** Sử dụng các công cụ tìm kiếm thời gian thực, truy xuất tài liệu khoa học (arXiv) và dữ liệu mạng xã hội để thu thập thông tin cập nhật nhất.
*   **Tổng hợp và Phân tích:** Chuyển đổi các dữ liệu phức tạp, rời rạc thành các báo cáo cô đọng, có cấu trúc rõ ràng (sử dụng bảng biểu, danh sách, tiêu đề).
*   **Xử lý thông tin chủ động:** Tự động xác định mục tiêu của người dùng, kiểm chứng chéo các nguồn tin và dự đoán các bước tiếp theo cần thiết để giải quyết vấn đề.
*   **Tuân thủ nghiêm ngặt:** Hoạt động dựa trên nguyên tắc "Không ảo tưởng" (Zero Hallucination), không đưa ra thông tin giả định và luôn minh bạch về các dữ liệu còn thiếu.

### Cách tôi làm việc:

1.  **BLUF (Bottom Line Up Front):** Đưa ra câu trả lời trực diện, quan trọng nhất ngay từ câu đầu tiên.
2.  **Cấu trúc rõ ràng:** Sử dụng định dạng Markdown, bảng so sánh và các mục liệt kê để tối ưu hóa khả năng đọc hiểu.
3.  **Tập trung vào giá trị:** Loại bỏ các nội dung rườm rà, không cần thiết để tiết kiệm thời gian cho người dùng.
4.  **Xác thực:** Chỉ cung cấp thông tin dựa trên các nguồn đáng tin cậy và tài liệu nội bộ được cung cấp.

**Bạn có thể yêu cầu tôi:**
*   Tìm kiếm và tóm tắt thông tin về một chủ đề cụ thể.
*   So sánh các lựa chọn, tính năng hoặc dữ liệu định lượng dưới dạng bảng.
*   Tra cứu các bài báo khoa học hoặc xu hướng mới nhất.
*   Tổng hợp dữ liệu từ nhiều nguồn để hỗ trợ lập kế hoạch hoặc ra quyết định.

**Tôi có thể giúp gì cho công việc của bạn ngay bây giờ?**

items.title: 19Network Introduces the UAE’s First AI-Powered Newsroom,Redefini - The National Law Review
items.summary: ### 19Network Introduces the UAE’s First AI-Powered Newsroom,Redefining Digital News for Business,Real Estate and AI Tech. *19Network launches an AI-powered newsroom covering UAE news, real estate, technology, business, lifestyle and global developments.*. DUBAI, UNITED ARAB EMIRATES, June 2, 2026 /EINPresswire.com/ -- 19Network.com, a UAE-based digital news and editorial platform, has introduced itself as the UAE’s first AI-powered newsroom, built to deliver fast, relevant and sector-focused coverage across the UAE, the wider GCC and international markets. Its core coverage areas include UAE news, real estate, artificial intelligence, technology, business, finance, lifestyle, travel, entertainment, sports, education, health and global developments. With its AI-powered newsroom direction, 19Network aims to provide a new model for digital publishing from the UAE, focused on speed, sector understanding and editorial relevance. As the UAE continues to strengthen its position as a global hub for business, investment, innovation, real estate, tourism and technology, the need for sharp, timely and multi-sector news coverage has become more important than ever.

items.title: $SOUN
items.summary:
$SOUN
A major challenge facing SoundHound AI (NASDAQ: SOUN) may be keeping pace with the tech industry's giants while simultaneously seeking to expand its own business. The fields of artificial intelligence (AI) and the voice AI services offered by SoundHound are intensely competitive. These concerns may be partly responsible for the stock's struggle to rebound this year; as of 2026, the share price has declined by approximately 10%. Nevertheless, SoundHound's management remains confident in its opportunities, and its CEO maintains that—unlike large tech companies—they do not need to overinvest or waste capital on AI.

items.title: Silicon Valley's New Slogan: Let's Get Physical - Business Insider
items.summary: You're currently following this topic! The latest news and analysis on robotics, from humanoid AI to real-world automation. ### Figure AI had one of its robots race an intern to sort packages. This weekend offered a glimpse of how quickly the biggest AI players are moving in on humanoid robotics. Robotics has become tech's latest arms race, with Nvidia, OpenAI, Meta, Tesla, and a flock of startups racing to give AI a body. "Physical AI" is the buzzword of the moment in the Valley, a term popularized by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to describe AI systems that can act in the physical world. One of the buzziest Silicon Valley robotics upstarts, Figure AI, has been trying to prove its technology can move from demos to real work. The team is teaching a robotic arm how to perform household tasks as part of an effort to build a humanoid robot, Business Insider reported earlier this year.

items.title: ANSCER Robotics closes Series A round for industrial material handling - The Robot Report
items.summary: # ANSCER Robotics closes Series A round for industrial material handling. ANSCER Robotics provides autonomous mobile robots and software for industrial material handling. Aiming to capture a larger share of the American logistics market, deep-tech startup ANSCER Robotics has raised $5.4 million (₹45 crore) in Series A funding to scale its fleet of hybrid warehouse robots globally. The company said it is designing and building automation for the realities of modern manufacturing and logistics by helping enterprises improve productivity, safety, and operational efficiency at scale. According to ANSCER Robotics, this latest investment will accelerate its mission to build India’s AI-native industrial automation platform for factories and warehouses worldwide. “Naturally, we are expanding our markets and our product line, and the company will have a booth at Automate 2026,” Mark Messina, CEO of ANSCER Robotics Americas, told *The Robot Report*. “Our robots are designed and built for deployment in complex, rough environments — manufacturing floors and dock areas.”. Mike Oitzman is Senior Editor of WTWH's Robotics Group and founder of the Mobile Robot Guide.

items.title: This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the ge
items.summary:
This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the generated file in your browser. I've also had some success asking the LLM to present its output as slideshows, etc.

More generally, imo audio is the human-preferred input to AIs but vision (images/animations/video) is the preferred output from them. Around a ~third of our brains are a massively parallel processor dedicated to vision, it is the 10-lane superhighway of information into brain. As AI improves, I think we'll see a progression that takes advantage:

1) raw text (hard/effortful to read)
2) markdown (bold, italic, headings, tables, a bit easier on the eyes) <-- current default
3) HTML (still procedural with underlying code, but a lot more flexibility on the graphics, layout, even interactivity) <-- early but forming new good default
...4,5,6,...
n) interactive neural videos/simulations

Imo the extrapolation (though the technology doesn't exist just yet) ends in some kind of interactive videos generated directly by a diffusion neural net. Many open questions as to how exact/procedural "Software 1.0" artifacts (e.g. interactive simulations) may be woven together with neural artifacts (diffusion grids), but generally something in the direction of the recently viral https://t.co/z21CP5iQfu

There are also improvements necessary and pending at the input. Audio nor text nor video alone are not enough, e.g. I feel a need to point/gesture to things on the screen, similar to all the things you would do with a person physically next to you and your computer screen.

TLDR The input/output mind meld between humans and AIs is ongoing and there is a lot of work to do and significant progress to be made, way before jumping all the way into neuralink-esque BCIs and all that. For what's worth exploring at the current stage, hot tip try ask for HTML.

items.title: Exclusive: OpenAI launches biodefense program - Axios
items.summary: # Exclusive: OpenAI launches biodefense program. OpenAI is launching a tool to help develop new biodefense and pandemic preparedness capabilities, according to an announcement shared first with Axios. **Driving the news:** OpenAI's new Rosalind Biodefense Program will offer its GPT-Rosalind model for life sciences research to "trusted developers" operationalizing biodefense tools. * The company will sponsor access to the tool and provide launch support for epidemiological modeling, early detection, screening, preparedness, non-pharmaceutical interventions and other public-health work. * OpenAI is supporting organizations that it says span the life cycle of biological threats "from prevention and early detection to societal resilience and medical countermeasure development.". **What's next:** OpenAI said it briefed the White House and several federal agencies on its approach, and is in the process of involving public-health-focused federal agencies. * The company is also expanding access to GPT-Rosalind for select U.S. government and allied partners to support public health and biodefense missions.

items.title: Exclusive: OpenAI readies cyber, misinformation defenses ahead of elections - Axios
items.summary: # Exclusive: OpenAI readies cyber, misinformation defenses ahead of elections. OpenAI is announcing new partnerships to combat misinformation, offering its cybersecurity products to state officials and backing legislation ahead of elections in the U.S. and globally. **Why it matters:** AI is becoming an election mainstay, with candidates, especially Republicans, using it for their campaigns as voters turn to chatbots for information. **Driving the news:** OpenAI is seeking to meet the moment with a series of new efforts shared first with Axios on Wednesday. * The company is offering its cybersecurity products — Codex Security and its Trusted Access for Cyber program — to registered voting system manufacturers in the U.S. * It's briefing the National Association of Secretaries of State and the National Association of State Election Directors on the latest cyber capabilities. * OpenAI made similar efforts ahead of the 2024 election, during which AI companies faced widespread criticism for inaccurate and misleading information being generated by chatbots.

items.title: OpenAI hit with lawsuit by Florida AG over AI risks - Politico
items.summary: # OpenAI hit with lawsuit by Florida AG over AI risks. It is the first such lawsuit to be filed by a state against OpenAI and Sam Altman. Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier filed a lawsuit on Monday against OpenAI and its chairman Sam Altman, alleging that the AI startup’s ChatGPT is unsafe. According to reports from the Wall Street Journal and NBC News, the suit contends that ChatGPT has provided assistance for mass shootings, and poses addiction and suicide risks to users. It is the first such product liability lawsuit to be filed against the company and its Chairman Sam Altman by a state. The Florida Attorney General’s office and OpenAI did not immediately respond to request for comment. This civil action is separate from a criminal investigation that Uthmeier opened into OpenAI in March after ChatGPT allegedly offered advice to the suspected gunman in a mass shooting at Florida State University that resulted in two deaths.

items.title: OpenAI heralds cybersecurity, election interference safeguard plans for 2026 midterms - CyberScoop
items.summary: The announcement builds on work from major tech firms in 2024 to combat AI-infused election chicanery. OpenAI on Wednesday hailed its plans to safeguard information and aid cybersecurity defenders in the 2026 midterm elections, including work to combat deepfakes and other forms of artificial intelligence misuse. The announcement builds on commitments from major tech companies in 2024, including OpenAI, to protect elections from AI-infused election interference — efforts that some thought weren’t enough. OpenAI highlighted that it has made its Codex Security agentic framework and Trusted Access for Cyber framework available to election officials, and was briefing the National Association of Secretaries of State and the National Association of State Election Directors on its tools. One new element of Wednesday’s announcement is that OpenAI has struck a partnership with the Associated Press on sharing election data.

items.title: Флорида штати OpenAI ва Sam Altman устидан судга шикоят киритди - Zamin.uz
items.summary: # Florida files lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman. The Florida Attorney General's Office has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman. This is the first state-level legal action regarding the involvement of the ChatGPT chatbot in cases of violence. The lawsuit accuses the company of neglecting safety measures in order to win the "AI race" and generate massive wealth. As part of a criminal investigation launched in April, the role of ChatGPT in a mass shooting that occurred last year at Florida State University (FSU) is being examined. OpenAI representatives deny responsibility for this tragedy, maintaining that ChatGPT is not the cause of the crime. This is not the only legal challenge for OpenAI. Additionally, a lawsuit recently filed by Elon Musk has concluded, in which the entrepreneur accused the company of abandoning its mission to benefit humanity and becoming a commercial organization. News » Technology » Florida files lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman.
